nodes.title: agent task (AT)
nodes.summary: A precise, checkable spec an autonomous coding agent runs to do one bounded piece of work, halting rather than improvising when it meets a surprise.

nodes.title: arc
nodes.summary: The full sequence of steps that carries a project from first idea to running system.

nodes.title: Basis of Design
nodes.summary: The one file that holds a project's settled design values as explicit numbers, the source of truth everything else builds from.

nodes.title: builds-on
nodes.summary: A frontmatter field naming the upstream documents a piece of work depends on, so opening it pulls its context in order.

nodes.title: closure
nodes.summary: The moment a unit of work and its look-back are finished and its status is marked done.

nodes.title: compressed chain
nodes.summary: The chain of up-front framing documents when a seasoned practitioner has deliberately merged or skipped some of its layers.

nodes.title: constitutive child
nodes.summary: A child unit of work that only makes sense alongside the parent it was split from.

nodes.title: dandori (段取り)
nodes.summary: The preparation that turns a resolved plan into exact, runnable instructions before any building starts.

nodes.title: declared compression
nodes.summary: Deliberately merging or skipping framing layers, but only when you write down what you combined and why, where the next session will see it.

nodes.title: design tuning
nodes.summary: Settling a design's feel by building throwaway try-outs, watching real values move, and locking the ones that feel right.

nodes.title: devlog
nodes.summary: A structured write-up done after a session that records what was decided and learned, so the work reads as yours rather than the machine's.

nodes.title: dogfood
nodes.summary: Learning a tool by actually using it on a real task, the way a real user would.

nodes.title: forward-only
nodes.summary: Finished work stays finished; a mistake is answered with a new document, never by editing the old one.

nodes.title: eval (graded eval)
nodes.summary: A human-graded check of output quality — correctness, clarity, judgment — where a pass/fail machine won't do.

nodes.title: ha (破)
nodes.summary: The middle stage where the practitioner breaks from the set form to scope and decide their own work.

nodes.title: ho (歩)
nodes.summary: One bounded, deliverable session of work that leaves a record when it closes.

nodes.title: ho 0.5
nodes.summary: The decimal slot for an unplanned step slipped in before the next major one.

nodes.title: ho-00
nodes.summary: A project's first session, which sets up its conditions, primers, and conventions and passes to the first building step.

nodes.title: ho document
nodes.summary: The written scope for one working session — its decisions, boundaries, and checks — read by the person doing the work.

nodes.title: ho overview
nodes.summary: The build's directional plan, grouping the planned steps into themed clusters with checkpoints and named milestones.

nodes.title: ho-process/
nodes.summary: The project folder that holds the methodology's working artifacts — the framing documents, the session records, and the task specs.

nodes.title: interaction test
nodes.summary: A human at a real terminal checking that something works and feels right, not just that the code runs.

nodes.title: kagen (加減)
nodes.summary: The felt-right amount for a value — its correct degree, found by feel rather than by rule.

nodes.title: Kamae (構え)
nodes.summary: The ready stance and all the framing done before a build, producing a chain of documents that move from opinions to a session plan.

nodes.title: Kamae addendum (kamae-N.M)
nodes.summary: A follow-on decision document that overrides a named part of an earlier framing document without editing it, carrying its own justification and record.

nodes.title: kokoroe (心得)
nodes.summary: The built-in care an executing agent works under — read the context first, treat the spec as its authority, check by command, and stop and surface on surprise.

nodes.title: landing ho
nodes.summary: A session whose whole job is settling visual or behavioral values at their felt-right degree and locking them with a commit.

nodes.title: learning walkthrough
nodes.summary: A plain-language, after-the-fact walk through what a session actually built, written for the practitioner as learner.

nodes.title: mind / hand
nodes.summary: The core principle of splitting one idea into its thinking (the mind) and its doing (the hand), so the doing can be handed off cheaply.

nodes.title: mutability (living / frozen / sealed)
nodes.summary: The scale of how much an artifact may change over its life — living (revised freely), frozen (changed only by override), or sealed (never again).

nodes.title: notes/
nodes.summary: The folder where findings gathered before a session wait until some later session pulls them in.

nodes.title: orientation ho
nodes.summary: A framing-only session — setting conditions, primers, and handoff — with no building work inside it.

nodes.title: parti
nodes.summary: The single core idea a whole project is organized around, the reference every later decision is judged against.

nodes.title: phase
nodes.summary: A named stage of work, used at three scales — the whole project's progression, a cluster of related sessions, and the steps within a single session.

nodes.title: precedential thinking
nodes.summary: Studying what already exists in a problem space before proposing to build anything new in it.

nodes.title: propagation ledger
nodes.summary: The running log of changes to the settled values, each commit naming why the value moved.

nodes.title: Reflect
nodes.summary: The honest look back after the work: did the plan hold, what changed, what the next session carries.

nodes.title: architectural register / executable register
nodes.summary: The two levels a single thought is written at: the reasoning a person reads, and the exact instructions the executor follows.

nodes.title: release tag
nodes.summary: A tagged commit marking the end of a build stage, forcing a done/not-done call and leaving a rollback point.

nodes.title: replan checkpoint
nodes.summary: A named pause in the plan where the practitioner stops, weighs real evidence, and decides whether to continue, insert, or rethink.

nodes.title: ri (離)
nodes.summary: The stage where the form has been so internalized the practitioner moves past it, working from mastery rather than rules.

nodes.title: seed
nodes.summary: The founding document that states a project's problem, vision, constraints, and guiding idea, revised deliberately as it evolves.

nodes.title: shape
nodes.summary: The structural form a session document takes, chosen at authoring time, describing the document rather than the person.

nodes.title: shu (守)
nodes.summary: The beginner stage of following a prescribed form exactly, before earning the judgment to depart from it.

nodes.title: shu-ha-ri (守破離)
nodes.summary: The three-stage path of mastery — follow the form, break from it deliberately, then transcend it — held separately for each skill.

nodes.title: sidecar
nodes.summary: A record kept in a sibling folder outside a fork's working tree, so your own process files never mix into someone else's repository.

nodes.title: sidequest
nodes.summary: A bounded, detachable line of work inside a project, with its own folder, numbering, and its own check that it worked.

nodes.title: smoke test
nodes.summary: An agent walking through the real feature to confirm it works — the floor other checks must clear, never the final word.

nodes.title: spike (design)
nodes.summary: A purpose-built proof that a design's settled values hold up against real code constraints, made as the reference the production build copies from.

nodes.title: splits-from
nodes.summary: A frontmatter field on a session carved out of an oversized one, pointing back at the original it was divided from.

nodes.title: standalone agent task
nodes.summary: A self-contained agent task that belongs to no larger session — maintenance, a surgical fix, or early exploration.

nodes.title: supersedes
nodes.summary: Names exactly what a newer document overtakes; the older one keeps a pointer back, and nothing is erased.

nodes.title: Think / Execute / Reflect
nodes.summary: The fixed three-part rhythm of a building session — decide first, build second, then judge honestly what happened.

nodes.title: tiered understanding
nodes.summary: Naming honestly how well you grasp something, on a three-step scale from use-it-blindly to could-rebuild-it.

nodes.title: tripwired
nodes.summary: Built to run on its own within set bounds but stop and raise a flag the instant it meets something unexpected.

nodes.title: tuner
nodes.summary: A live control that lets you move a visual or behavioral value and watch it change before committing to a number.

nodes.title: verification / validation
nodes.summary: The two halves of quality — verification asks whether it was built right, validation asks whether the right thing was built and whether it's any good.

nodes.title: What the Ho System Is
nodes.summary: Building production software with AI while developing real judgment.
